pi_items.source: Task description explicitly specifies the location as Saint-Rémy-de-Provence, France.
Task description explicitly specifies this coordinate/altitude value (43.789).
Task description explicitly specifies this coordinate/altitude value (4.831).
Calculated via math.radians(43.789) -> 0.764262... and cross-referenced with verifier.py constants.
Calculated via math.radians(4.831) -> 0.084316... (truncated to 0.08431) and cross-referenced with verifier.py constants.
Task description explicitly specifies this coordinate/altitude value (60).
Task description specifies June 19, 1889 at 04:30 UTC.
Task description specifies June 19, 1889 at 04:30 UTC.
Task description instructs: Atmosphere ON, Ground/landscape OFF, Constellation lines ON, Planet labels ON. Matched via verifier.py checking these exact flags from Stellarium config.ini.
Task description requests file at ~/Desktop/starry_night_analysis.txt and export_result.sh checks /home/ga/Desktop/starry_night_analysis.txt.
export_result.sh counts screenshots in /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium, which is where Stellarium saves screenshots by default and is specified in the prompt.
Task description explicitly asks to search for Venus and Aldebaran.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on Stellarium's simulation of real astronomical events. The target coordinates (Saint-Rémy-de-Provence: 43.789°N, 4.831°E) and altitude (60m) exactly match the prompt's instructions and convert to the radians expected by the verifier (0.76426, 0.08431). The required date/time (June 19, 1889 04:30 UTC) and Stellarium display flags (atmosphere ON, landscape OFF, constellation lines ON, planet labels ON) are explicitly enforced by the verifier. The output paths for screenshots (/home/ga/Pictures/stellarium) and analysis report (/home/ga/Desktop/starry_night_analysis.txt) are statically confirmed via export and verifier scripts. The celestial targets (Venus and Aldebaran) match the prompt instructions.
